subject: Code Smell Scan —SquadDash-public
body:
## Code Smell Scan — SquadDash-public

Branch: `maintenance/20260524-code-smells`  
Scanned: `SquadDash/` (C# WPF), `Squad.SDK/` (TypeScript), `VoiceHeuristics/`  
**Total findings: 57** across 4 categories of concern.

---

## 🔴 High Priority

These have the widest blast radius or make the code hardest to change safely.

### God Classes

| File | Anchor | Description | Suggested Fix |
|------|--------|-------------|---------------|
| `SquadDash/MainWindow.xaml.cs` | `MainWindow` class | 250+ fields and40+ private services coordinating transcript UI, workspace, background tasks, preferences, approval, notes, maintenance, and inbox — all in one class. | Extract focused managers: `TranscriptUIManager`, `WorkspaceManager`, `BackgroundTaskManager`, `SettingsPanelManager`. Each owns a single domain.|
| `SquadDash/TranscriptConversationManager.cs` | `TranscriptConversationManager` | 40+ fields managing conversation state, persistence, prompt history, session tracking, lazy-render caching, virtual window scrolling, and thread snapshots. | Split into `ConversationStatePersistence`, `PromptHistoryManager`, `VirtualScrollManager`, `AgentThreadRenderCache`. |

### Constructor Over-Injection

| File | Anchor | Description | Suggested Fix |
|------|--------|-------------|---------------|
| `SquadDash/PromptExecutionController.cs` | Constructor | Takes 30+ delegate/function parameters instead of injecting composed services. Makes instantiation, testing, and dependency tracing painful. | Create a `PromptExecutionServices` record to bundle related callbacks. |
| `SquadDash/AgentThreadRegistry.cs` | Constructor | 18 delegate parameters for rendering, syncing, and lifecycle callbacks — all UI concerns passed as loose functions. | Create `ThreadRenderingCallbacks` and `ThreadLifecycleCallbacks` records to group related delegates. |

### Long Methods

| File | Anchor | Description | Suggested Fix |
|------|--------|-------------|---------------|
| `SquadDash/PushNotificationService.cs` | `ExtractGitCommitInfo` | 47-line method with 4sequential priority-based regex searches and nested loops. | Extract each priority to its own method: `TryExtractPriority1_GitNativeWithMessage()`, etc. |
| `Squad.SDK/runPrompt.ts` | `main()` | 240-line main loop with 18+ conditional branches handling different request types. Adding or removing a request type is error-prone. | Use a handler registry: `Map<RequestType, Handler>` iterated instead of a flat if-chain. |
| `Squad.SDK/runPrompt.ts` | `buildRunHandlers` | 165-line function creating a9-callback handler object, mixing RC bridge logic with generic event emission. | Split into `createStdoutHandlers()` and `createRcHandlers()` to isolate RC concerns. |
| `Squad.SDK/squadService.ts` | `loadBackgroundTasks` | 97-line method handling task fetching, progress loading, normalization, and merging in one function. | Split into `fetchBackgroundTasks()`, `buildBackgroundAgentInfo()`, `buildBackgroundShellInfo()`. |

---

## 🟠Duplicate Code

### C# Duplicates

| File | Anchor | Description | Suggested Fix |
|------|--------|-------------|---------------|
| `SquadDash/JsonFileStorage.cs` | `AtomicWrite<T>` / `AtomicWrite(string)` | Two nearly-identical methods (temp file write, replace/copy fallback) differ only in the serialization step. | Extract `AtomicWriteCore(string tempPath, string finalPath)` and have both overloads call it. |
| `SquadDash/MaintenanceRunner.cs` | `BuildPrompt`, `BuildInboxRecoveryPrompt` | Both construct similar `INBOX_MESSAGE_JSON` block templates with the same repeated field definitions. | Extract `BuildInboxMessageTemplate()` used by both. |
| `SquadDash/InboxStore.cs` | `MarkRead`, `MarkUnread` | Identical pattern: lock → load → mutate one property → serialize → write. Only the property name differs. | Extract `UpdateMessageProperty(string id, Func<InboxMessage, InboxMessage> mutator)`. |
| `SquadDash/ToolTranscriptFormatter.cs` | `GetToolEmoji` / `GetToolEmojiByName` | Two nearly-identical switch statements differing only in the guard condition logic. | Consolidate into one method with an optional parameter. |
| `SquadDash/ColorUtilities.cs` | `AccentBrush()` / `CreateAccentBrush()` | Two methods with identical implementations. | Remove the duplicate; keep one canonical name. |
| `SquadDash/SquadTeamRosterLoader.cs` | `GetMissingUtilityAgentNames` / `Load` | Both independently construct the same folder paths (`normalizedWorkspace`, `squadFolder`, `agentsFolder`). | Extract path setup to a shared private helper. |
| `SquadDash/BackgroundTaskStateResolver.cs` | `IsThreadBackedBySnapshot` | Three nearly-identical `if` blocks check different ID fields via `snapshotAgents.Any()`. | Extract into a helper accepting a lambda selector for the ID field. |
| `SquadDash/AzureSpeechRecognitionService.cs` | `StartAsync` / `StartFromStreamAsync` | SpeechConfig creation, phraseList grammar, and event subscriptions are duplicated verbatim in both methods. | Extract common setup into `private InitializeRecognizer()`. |
| `SquadDash/SpeechRecognitionService.cs` + `WhisperSpeechRecognitionService.cs` | `OnAudioData` | RMS/volume calculation duplicated verbatim across both files; magic number `6000.0` appears in both. | Extract to a shared `VolumeCalculator` utility class. |
| `SquadDash/SquadRuntimeCompatibility.cs` | `PatchVscodeJsonrpcExports` / `PatchCopilotSdkSessionImport` | Both follow identical pattern: path check → read/parse → search → write back with logging. | Extract a patch template: `ApplyPatch(packagePath, searchKey, patchFactory) → bool`. |
| `SquadDash/MaintenanceMdParser.cs` + `LoopMdParser.cs` | YAML parsing sections | Nearly identical indentation-based YAML parsing code duplicated across both files. | Extract a shared `YamlFrontmatterParser` with `ParseKeyValue`, `ParseIndentedBlock`, `CountIndent` static methods. |
| `SquadDash/SquadCliAdapter.cs` | `LaunchPowerShellCommandWindow` | `completionMessage`/`failureMessage` are set via a switch on `action.Label` with hardcoded strings. New actions require editing in multiple places. | Create a `PowerShellActionDescriptor` record looked up by label. |

### TypeScript Duplicates

| File | Anchor | Description | Suggested Fix |
|------|--------|-------------|---------------|
| `Squad.SDK/squadService.ts` | `getEventStringValue`, `getEventNumberValue`, `getEventBooleanValue`, etc. | Multiple nearly-identical functions extract a value from either root or `.data` property; only the type check differs. | Extract `getEventValue<T>(event, name, extractor)` generic helper. |
| `Squad.SDK/squadService.ts` | `agentInfoEqual` / `shellInfoEqual` | Two manual property-by-property equality functions for different types following the same pattern. | Use a generic deep-equal utility or a comparer factory. |
| `Squad.SDK/squadService.ts` | `findPackageRoot` / `findCopilotPackageRoot` | Two nearly-identical walk-up-directory-tree functions; only the search candidates differ. | Extract shared traversal into one helper accepting a predicate. |
| `Squad.SDK/squadService.ts` | `describeBackgroundCancelState` / `cancelBackgroundTask` | Candidate-state filtering and prioritization algorithm duplicated between the two methods. | Extract `selectCandidateStates(sessionId, taskId, allStates)` used by both. |
| `Squad.SDK/runPrompt.ts` | `tryParsePromptRequest`, `tryParseDelegateRequest`, etc. (5 functions) | All five follow identical pattern: validate required fields, trim/validate strings, extract optional fields, return typed object. | Create `createRequestParser(requiredFields, optionalFields)` factory. |

---

## 🟡 Complex Conditionals & Poor Readability

| File | Anchor | Category | Description | Suggested Fix |
|------|--------|----------|-------------|---------------|
| `SquadDash/MarkdownFlowDocumentBuilder.cs` | `Build` method | Complex Conditional | 50+ sequential `if (trimmed.StartsWith(...))` checks for every Markdown element type. Hard to extend. | Create `IMarkdownElementParser` implementations (CodeBlockParser, TableParser, etc.) iterated in a chain. |
| `SquadDash/WorkspaceConversationStore.cs` | `Save` method | Complex Conditional | Chain of defensive checks (`WouldOverwriteNonEmptyState`, `WouldDropDurableTranscriptContent`, `IsExplicitClear`, `HasMeaningfulContent`) is hard to follow. | Extract to a `ConversationSaveValidator` class with named validation methods. |
| `SquadDash/InboxMessageParser.cs` | JSON extraction loop | Complex Conditional | Manual JSON brace-matching with escape tracking (`escaped` flag, string state, depth counter) — hard to verify correctness and easy to break. | Delegate to `JsonDocument.Parse()`; find marker, then let the parser handle nesting and escapes. |
| `SquadDash/AttachmentBlockFormatter.cs` | `StripTypedAttachmentHeaders` | Complex Conditional | Multiple nested if-statements checking blank lines, recognized headers, and XML blocks simultaneously. | Extract `TryConsumeBlankLineOrBreak()` and similar helpers to name each decision. |
| `SquadDash/PushNotificationService.cs` | `NotificationRateLimiter.ShouldSend` | Complex Conditional | Nested if/else escalation logic with unexplained magic numbers 3and 5for threshold checks. | Extract thresholds to named constants; consider a state-machine for escalation levels. |
| `Squad.SDK/squadService.ts` | `collectSchemaConstValues` | Complex Conditional | Recursive tree traversal with nested `$ref` resolution, array iteration, and reference tracking all interleaved. | Break into `resolveRef()`, `traverseArray()`, `collectConsts()` helper functions. |
| `Squad.SDK/runPrompt.ts` | `startTunnel` | Complex Conditional | 70lines with nested promises, polling loops, and near-identical ngrok vs cloudflare branches differing only in regex and error messages. | Extract `createNgrokTunnel()` and `createCloudflareTunnel()` with shared `waitForTunnelUrl()`. |

---

## 🟢 Lower Priority (Naming, Magic Values, Patterns)

### Missing Null Checks / Error Context

| File | Anchor | Description | Suggested Fix |
|------|--------|-------------|---------------|
| `SquadDash/TriggeredPromptInjection.cs` | `TriggeredInjectionEvaluator.Evaluate` | Variable replacement loop calls `Replace()` without validating that `value` is non-null. | Add `if (value is not null)` guard before the dictionary iteration. |
| `SquadDash/OpenAiTtsProvider.cs` | `SpeakAsync` | `MediaPlayer` `Open`/`Play` never checked for failure; temp file is not cleaned up if playback throws. | Add error handler; implement cleanup callback or use `try/finally`. |
| `SquadDash/LoopController.cs` | Constructor | `_onBeforeIteration` and `_onBeforeWait` use null-coalescing defaults; other callbacks in the same constructor do not. Inconsistent null-safety. | Consistently use `??(() => Task.CompletedTask)` for all optional callbacks, or use explicit `ArgumentNullException` for required ones. |
| `SquadDash/ProcessIdentity.cs` | `GetCurrentProcessStartedAtUtcTicks` | Empty `catch` block returns a fallback with no logging — callers cannot diagnose process-lookup failures. | Add `SquadDashTrace.Write` or `Debug.WriteLine` in the catch to document the reason for the fallback. |

### Magic Numbers & Strings

| File | Anchor | Description | Suggested Fix |
|------|--------|-------------|---------------|
| `SquadDash/BackgroundAgentLaunchInfoResolver.cs` | `ScoreCandidate` | Scoring constants120, 110, 100, 90, 70, 60have no documented rationale. | Define `const int ExactNameMatchScore = 120;` etc. with comments explaining relative weights. |
| `SquadDash/CurrentTurnStatusPresentation.cs` | `BuildDetail` | Thresholds `8`, `3`, `800` ms used without names or explanation. | Define `const int ThinkingLoopThreshold = 8;` etc. |
| `SquadDash/BackgroundWorkClassifier.cs` | `IsPlanningWork` | Hardcoded phrase strings searched in concatenated text with no explanation of why these phrases indicate planning. | Define named constants for each phrase with documentation. |
| `SquadDash/PushNotificationService.cs` | `BuildFallbackSummary` | 70+ hardcoded stop-words inline; `Take(7)` word limit unexplained. | Move stop words to a `static readonly HashSet`; define `const int FallbackSummaryTokens = 7`. |
| `SquadDash/MaintenanceRunner.cs` | `BuildPrompt` | Safety-level strings `"report-only"`, `"branch"`, `"direct"` appear as magic strings in comparisons. | Extract as `const string SafetyReportOnly = "report-only";` etc. |
| `Squad.SDK/runPrompt.ts` | `startTunnel` | Timeouts `20_000` (Cloudflare) and `12000` (ngrok) hardcoded without explanation. | Define `const CLOUDFLARE_TUNNEL_TIMEOUT_MS = 20_000` and `NGROK_TUNNEL_TIMEOUT_MS = 12_000` with comments. |
| `Squad.SDK/squadService.ts` | Module top-level | `60* 60 * 1000` session idle timeout computed inline with no label. | Extract `const SESSION_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MS = 60 * 60 * 1000; // 1 hour`. |

### Poor / Unclear Naming

| File | Anchor | Description | Suggested Fix |
|------|--------|-------------|---------------|
| `SquadDash/QuickReplyOptionParser.cs` | `StripTrailingPartialOption` | Name doesn't convey it truncates at an unclosed `[`. Behavior is invisible from the name. | Rename to `TruncateAtUnclosedBracket()` or add XML doc explaining mid-transmission truncation. |
| `SquadDash/TasksPanelParser.cs` | `Parse` — `inCompletedSection` | Variable name is vague; emoji normalization (🔵→🟢) is a silent side-effect. | Rename to `isCompletedItemsSection`; extract `NormalizePriorityEmoji()` to make the mapping explicit. |
| `SquadDash/SquadRoutingDocumentService.cs` | `IsUntouchedSeed` | 8separate `.Contains()` checks on `normalized` with no clear naming pattern for seed templates. | Extract seed template strings to a `static readonly string[]` constant; add XML doc on what "untouched seed" means. |
| `SquadDash/SquadBridgePromptBuilder.cs` | `HiringIntentRegexes`, `HiringFalsePositiveRegexes` | Individual regex entries have no comments explaining what each pattern matches or why. | Add XML doc above each regex. |

### Inefficient Patterns

| File | Anchor | Description | Suggested Fix |
|------|--------|-------------|---------------|
| `SquadDash/MarkdownFlowDocumentBuilder.cs` | `AddTextWithCircleEmoji` | Loop searches the entire emoji dictionary on every character. | Pre-compute emoji positions or use an indexed approach. |
| `SquadDash/AgentThreadIdentityPolicy.cs` | `NormalizeKey` | Builds a new `StringBuilder` on every call to filter characters; called frequently. | Replace with `string.Concat(input.Where(c => IsAllowed(c)))` or pre-compile a `Regex` for stripping. |
| `SquadDash/PromptQueue.cs` | `Reorder` | Removes an item and reinserts it; no existence check before the clamp; silent truncation has no documentation. | Check `FindIndex` first; document why the clamp is applied. |
| `SquadDash/SoundNotificationService.cs` | `GetEventSettings` | Long switch with8 repetitive property lookups; adding a new event requires edits in multiple places. | Create a dictionary mapping `SoundEvent` to property-name pairs. |
| `SquadDash/IdleDetectionService.cs` | `IsThresholdElapsed` | `_thresholdMinutes * 60_000.0` computed identically in two methods with no shared definition. | Extract to a cached property or named constant. |
| `SquadDash/RunningInstanceRegistry.cs` | `SaveCore` | Copy+Delete for existing file vs. Move for new file — inconsistent I/O patterns in the same method. | Consolidate to atomic `WriteAllText` + `File.Move` with a single exception handler. |

### Dead Code

| File | Anchor | Description | Suggested Fix |
|------|--------|-------------|---------------|
| `Squad.SDK/index.ts` | entire file | File contains only `console.log('Hello world');` — a placeholder left in the production SDK. | Delete or replace with proper module exports (`export * from './squadService';`). |
| `Squad.SDK/runPrompt.ts` | BYOK diagnostic block | Large block of commented-out diagnostic code (~9 lines). | Remove; document BYOK diagnostic needs in a `.md` file or enable via an env-var check. |

### Other

| File | Anchor | Description | Suggested Fix |
|------|--------|-------------|---------------|
| `Squad.SDK/squadService.ts` | `(remoteBridge as any)` (multiple call sites) | Multiple `as any` casts to call `broadcast()`, which is not in the `RemoteBridge` type definition. | Extend the `RemoteBridgeWithBroadcast` interface to include `broadcast()`; eliminate `any`. |
| `Squad.SDK/package.json` | `scripts` section | `eslint` and related packages are in `devDependencies` but no `lint` script exists; lint is never run in the build. | Add `"lint": "eslint src/**/*.ts"` and integrate into CI. |
| `SquadDash/AgentCardSorting.cs` | `ComputeSortKey` | Takes6 parameters,5 of which are related boolean flags. | Replace booleans with a `[Flags]` enum or a parameter object. |
| `SquadDash/AgentStatusCard.cs` | `ImagesVisible` | Static property directly mutates live instances via weak references and manually fires property-changed notifications — mixing static state management with instance concerns. | Move to a dedicated visibility manager or an event-driven state container. |
| `SquadDash/MutexLease.cs` | `TryAcquire` | Static `ConcurrentDictionary` with manual thread-key composition adds complexity beyond what standard `Mutex` reentrancy provides. | Evaluate whether the static tracking adds value; if not, simplify to standard `Mutex.WaitOne`. |
| `SquadDash/SquadBridgePromptBuilder.cs` | `BuildResult` | Uses a local static function `AppendSegment()` defined inside the method — unusual pattern in this codebase. | Promote to a private static class method for consistency and testability. |

actions.label: Fix God Classes
actions.prompt:
Arjun: a code smell scan on2026-05-24 (branch: maintenance/20260524-code-smells) found two God Classes that are the highest-priority structural issues inSquadDash-public.

1. **`SquadDash/MainWindow.xaml.cs` — `MainWindow` class**: Has 250+ fields and 40+ private services managing transcript UI, workspace, background tasks, preferences, approval panel, tasks panel, notes, maintenance, and inbox — all in one class. Suggested decomposition: `TranscriptUIManager`, `WorkspaceManager`, `BackgroundTaskManager`, `SettingsPanelManager`, each owning a single domain.

2. **`SquadDash/TranscriptConversationManager.cs` — `TranscriptConversationManager`**: 40+ fields managing conversation state, persistence, prompt history, session tracking, lazy-render caching, virtual scroll, and thread snapshots. Suggested split: `ConversationStatePersistence`, `PromptHistoryManager`, `VirtualScrollManager`, `AgentThreadRenderCache`.

Please analyse these two classes in detail, design a safe decomposition strategy that doesn't break the existing test suite (SquadDash.Tests/), and implement the refactoring on the branch `maintenance/20260524-code-smells`. Run `dotnet test` to verify nothing is broken. Commit with the trailer: Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

actions.label: Fix constructor over-injection
actions.prompt:
Arjun: a code smell scan on 2026-05-24 (branch: maintenance/20260524-code-smells) found two constructors with excessive delegate injection in SquadDash-public.

1. **`SquadDash/PromptExecutionController.cs` constructor**: Takes 30+ delegate/function parameters. Suggested fix: create a `PromptExecutionServices` record to bundle related callbacks, and update the constructor and all call sites.

2. **`SquadDash/AgentThreadRegistry.cs` constructor**: Takes 18 delegate parameters for rendering, syncing, and lifecycle callbacks. Suggested fix: create `ThreadRenderingCallbacks` and `ThreadLifecycleCallbacks` records; update the constructor and all call sites.

Please implement both refactorings on branch `maintenance/20260524-code-smells`, run `dotnet test` to verify nothing is broken, and commit. Trailer: Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
actions.hint: Bundle30+ and 18+ delegate params into parameter objects

actions.label: Fix duplicate code (C#)
actions.prompt:
Arjun: a code smell scan on 2026-05-24 (branch: maintenance/20260524-code-smells) found 12 duplicate-code sites in the C# codebase at SquadDash-public. Please fix them:

1. `SquadDash/JsonFileStorage.cs` — `AtomicWrite<T>` and `AtomicWrite(string)` share identical temp-file-write logic. Extract `AtomicWriteCore(string tempPath, string finalPath)`.
2. `SquadDash/MaintenanceRunner.cs` — `BuildPrompt` and `BuildInboxRecoveryPrompt` both construct INBOX_MESSAGE_JSON templates. Extract `BuildInboxMessageTemplate()`.
3. `SquadDash/InboxStore.cs` — `MarkRead` and `MarkUnread` differ only in property mutated. Extract `UpdateMessageProperty(string id, Func<InboxMessage, InboxMessage> mutator)`.
4. `SquadDash/ToolTranscriptFormatter.cs` — `GetToolEmoji` and `GetToolEmojiByName` are nearly-identical switches. Consolidate into one method.
5. `SquadDash/ColorUtilities.cs` — `AccentBrush()` and `CreateAccentBrush()` are identical. Remove the duplicate.
6. `SquadDash/SquadTeamRosterLoader.cs` — `GetMissingUtilityAgentNames` and `Load` duplicate path-construction code. Extract a shared path-setup helper.
7. `SquadDash/BackgroundTaskStateResolver.cs` — `IsThreadBackedBySnapshot` has three near-identical `if` blocks. Extract into a helper accepting a lambda selector.
8. `SquadDash/AzureSpeechRecognitionService.cs` — `StartAsync` and `StartFromStreamAsync` duplicate recognizer setup. Extract `InitializeRecognizer()`.
9. `SquadDash/SpeechRecognitionService.cs` + `WhisperSpeechRecognitionService.cs` — RMS/volume calculation duplicated verbatim (magic number 6000.0 in both). Extract to a shared `VolumeCalculator` utility.
10. `SquadDash/SquadRuntimeCompatibility.cs` — `PatchVscodeJsonrpcExports` and `PatchCopilotSdkSessionImport` share the same patch pattern. Extract `ApplyPatch(packagePath, searchKey, patchFactory)`.
11. `SquadDash/MaintenanceMdParser.cs` + `LoopMdParser.cs` — identical YAML parsing. Extract a shared `YamlFrontmatterParser`.
12. `SquadDash/SquadCliAdapter.cs` — `LaunchPowerShellCommandWindow` switches on `action.Label` for messages. Create a `PowerShellActionDescriptor` lookup.

Work on branch `maintenance/20260524-code-smells`. Run `dotnet test` after each group. Commit with trailer: Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

actions.label: Fix TypeScript smells
actions.prompt:
Arjun: a code smell scan on 2026-05-24 (branch: maintenance/20260524-code-smells) found multiple smells in the TypeScript SDK at SquadDash-public/Squad.SDK/. Please fix the following:

**Duplicate code:**
- `squadService.ts` — `getEventStringValue`, `getEventNumberValue`, `getEventBooleanValue` etc. are nearly-identical. Extract a generic `getEventValue<T>(event, name, extractor)` helper.
- `squadService.ts` — `agentInfoEqual` / `shellInfoEqual` are property-by-property equality duplicates. Use a comparer factory or deep-equal utility.
- `squadService.ts` — `findPackageRoot` / `findCopilotPackageRoot` duplicate directory-traversal logic. Extract shared helper accepting a predicate.
- `squadService.ts` — `describeBackgroundCancelState` / `cancelBackgroundTask` duplicate candidate-state filtering. Extract `selectCandidateStates(sessionId, taskId, allStates)`.
- `runPrompt.ts` — 5 `tryParse*Request` functions are structurally identical. Extract `createRequestParser(requiredFields, optionalFields)` factory.

**Long methods:**
- `runPrompt.ts` — `main()` (240lines, 18+ if-branches). Introduce a handler registry `Map<RequestType, Handler>`.
- `runPrompt.ts` — `buildRunHandlers` (165 lines). Split into `createStdoutHandlers()` and `createRcHandlers()`.
- `squadService.ts` — `loadBackgroundTasks` (97 lines). Split into `fetchBackgroundTasks()`, `buildBackgroundAgentInfo()`, `buildBackgroundShellInfo()`.

**Dead code:**
- `index.ts` — entire file is just `console.log('Hello world')`. Replace with proper module exports or delete.
- `runPrompt.ts` — large commented-out BYOK diagnostic block. Remove it.

**Type safety:**
- `squadService.ts` — multiple `(remoteBridge as any)` casts to call `broadcast()`. Extend the `RemoteBridge` interface to include `broadcast()` and remove the `any` casts.

**Magic numbers:**
- `runPrompt.ts` — timeouts `20_000` and `12000` in `startTunnel`. Define `CLOUDFLARE_TUNNEL_TIMEOUT_MS` and `NGROK_TUNNEL_TIMEOUT_MS`.
- `squadService.ts` — `60 * 60 * 1000` inline. Define `SESSION_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MS`.

Work on branch `maintenance/20260524-code-smells`. Run `npm test` in Squad.SDK/ after changes. Commit with trailer: Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
actions.hint: Duplicate code, long methods, dead code in Squad.SDK

actions.label: Fix lower-priority smells
actions.prompt:
Arjun: a code smell scan on 2026-05-24 (branch: maintenance/20260524-code-smells) found several lower-priority smells in SquadDash-public. Please address them:

**Missing null checks / error context:**
- `SquadDash/TriggeredPromptInjection.cs` — `TriggeredInjectionEvaluator.Evaluate`: dictionary value used in `Replace()` without null guard. Add `if (value is not null)`.
- `SquadDash/OpenAiTtsProvider.cs` — `SpeakAsync`: `MediaPlayer` open/play not checked; temp file not cleaned up on failure. Add error handler and `try/finally` cleanup.
- `SquadDash/LoopController.cs` — constructor: inconsistent null-coalescing; some optional callbacks have `?? default` but others do not. Apply `?? (() => Task.CompletedTask)` consistently.
- `SquadDash/ProcessIdentity.cs` — `GetCurrentProcessStartedAtUtcTicks`: empty catch swallows failures silently. Add `SquadDashTrace.Write` or `Debug.WriteLine`.

**Magic numbers / strings:**
- `SquadDash/BackgroundAgentLaunchInfoResolver.cs` — `ScoreCandidate`: scoring constants 120/110/100/90/70/60 with no rationale. Define named constants with comments.
- `SquadDash/CurrentTurnStatusPresentation.cs` — `BuildDetail`: thresholds 8, 3, 800ms unnamed. Define named constants.
- `SquadDash/PushNotificationService.cs` — `BuildFallbackSummary`: 70+ inline stop-words; `Take(7)` unexplained. Move to `static readonly HashSet`; define `const int FallbackSummaryTokens = 7`.
- `SquadDash/MaintenanceRunner.cs` — `BuildPrompt`: safety-level strings `"report-only"`, `"branch"`, `"direct"` as magic strings. Define constants.

**Poor naming:**
- `SquadDash/QuickReplyOptionParser.cs` — `StripTrailingPartialOption`: rename to `TruncateAtUnclosedBracket()` or add XML doc.
- `SquadDash/TasksPanelParser.cs` — `inCompletedSection` → `isCompletedItemsSection`; extract `NormalizePriorityEmoji()`.
- `SquadDash/SquadRoutingDocumentService.cs` — `IsUntouchedSeed`: extract template strings to a named array.

**Inefficient patterns:**
- `SquadDash/AgentThreadIdentityPolicy.cs` — `NormalizeKey`: rebuilds `StringBuilder` per call. Use `string.Concat(input.Where(...))` or a compiled `Regex`.
- `SquadDash/PromptQueue.cs` — `Reorder`: no existence check before clamp; document why clamp is applied.
- `SquadDash/SoundNotificationService.cs` — `GetEventSettings`: long switch for property lookups. Use a `Dictionary<SoundEvent, ...>`.
- `SquadDash/IdleDetectionService.cs` — `IsThresholdElapsed`: `_thresholdMinutes * 60_000.0` duplicated. Extract to property.

**Other:**
- `SquadDash/AgentCardSorting.cs` — `ComputeSortKey`: 5 boolean flags as params. Replace with a `[Flags]` enum or parameter object.
- `SquadDash/SquadBridgePromptBuilder.cs` — `BuildResult`: local static `AppendSegment()` inside method. Promote to private static class method.
- `Squad.SDK/package.json` — no `lint` script despite ESLint being in devDependencies. Add `"lint": "eslint src/**/*.ts"` and wire into CI.

Work on branch `maintenance/20260524-code-smells`. Run `dotnet test` after C# changes. Commit with trailer: Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
actions.hint: Naming, magic values, missing null checks, inefficient patterns

actions.label: Dismiss
actions.hint: Acknowledge the report — no action will be taken
